title: Error: wrong number of file descriptors for socket activation protocol (2 != 1)
body:
### Issue Description

Point of @rhatdan in issue #17754 that team 

> ... doesn't want to support two sockets at the same time...

sounds quite reasonable but breaks systemd socket activation if we use TCP connection to podman instead of UNIX socket.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install podman and make two .socket units -- with UNIX socket and with TCP port 2375 just to be compatible with Docker.
2. Try to use, f.e, `podman -r --uri unix:///run/podman/podman.sock ps`
3. Try to use `podman -r --uri tcp://localhost:2375 ps`


### Describe the results you received

First invocation will success, and you will see your containers running locally (or nothing).
Second invocation will hang flooding journal with:
`DATE` `HOST` podman[`PID`]: time="`TIMESTAMP`" level=info msg="/usr/bin/podman filtering at log level info"
`DATE` `HOST` podman[`PID`]: time="`TIMESTAMP`" level=info msg="Setting parallel job count to 13"
`DATE` `HOST` podman[`PID`]: time="`TIMESTAMP`" level=info msg="Using systemd socket activation to determine API endpoint"
`DATE` `HOST` podman[`PID`]: Error: wrong number of file descriptors for socket activation protocol (2 != 1)
`DATE` `HOST` systemd[1]: podman.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=125/n/a


### Describe the results you expected

Obviously, results ought to be the same.

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Just for clarification:

**host# cat /etc/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/podman.socket** 
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman API Socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)

[Socket]
ListenStream=%t/podman/podman.sock
SocketMode=0660

[Install]
WantedBy=sockets.target
```

**host# cat /etc/systemd/system/sockets.target.wants/podman-tcp.socket** 
```
[Unit]
Description=Podman non-TLS TCP/IP API Socket
Documentation=man:podman-system-service(1)

[Socket]
ListenStream=2375
Service=podman.service

[Install]
WantedBy=sockets.target

```

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Well you need to use it either or, you cannot use both the unix socket and tcp socket unit with the same service.
So you should replace the unix socket unit or create a second podman-tcp.service for the tcp socket unit.

Note that exposing the socket via TCP is not recommend at all, this allows RCE without any access control.

O.k, split-setup works while being... o.k, unobvious, uncommon and somehow contradictory with socket activation model itself, resembling old-fashioned inetd(8)-style service activation model.

> Note that exposing the socket via TCP is not recommend at all, this allows RCE without any access control.

Yes. In real life I use `ListenStream=127.0.0.1:2375`.

> Yes. In real life I use ListenStream=localhost:2375.

While better it still allows any user on the host to connect to it without access controls, also see https://proofnet.de/publikationen/podman_tcp_api.html that this can still lead to RCE.

---

If you really need to sockets for the same service then your welcome to send patches. There are problems around the inactivity timer and when to shutdown to avoid race conditions that is why I said it complicates things.
